findings.description: StreamingMedian.add uses insort into a list, which is O(n) per insertion and O(n^2) overall, despite the 'Streaming' name implying sub-linear (e.g. dual-heap) cost.
moving_average maintains an incremental running sum, so floating-point rounding error accumulates across the window slide versus recomputing each window sum.
running_total seeds the accumulator with 0.0, coercing integer inputs to float in the output list, which may be surprising for integer-typed streams.
clamp does not define behavior for a NaN value, where max(low, min(nan, high)) yields an implementation-dependent result that is silently returned.
safe_mean can return NaN or inf if the input contains NaN/inf values, with no documentation of that propagation in the contract.
